evals.prompt:
I need a freshly tailored resume. Resume: resume.pdf. Job: Senior Data Engineer at Stripe.

Requirements:
- 5+ years of experience in data engineering or backend engineering
- Strong proficiency in Python and SQL
- Experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines at scale
- Familiarity with distributed data processing frameworks (Spark, Flink, or Beam)
- Experience with cloud data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake)
- Strong understanding of data modeling and schema design
- BS/MS in Computer Science or equivalent

Nice to have:
- Experience with streaming data systems (Kafka, Kinesis)
- Familiarity with dbt or similar transformation tools
- Experience in fintech or payments industry
- Contributions to open-source data tools
evals.expected_output: A new versioned PDF file (e.g., tailored_resume_stripe_data_engineer_v1.pdf) saved to ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/. Chat output with exactly three sections: STRENGTHS, GAPS, SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS. No Key Changes section. Original resume.pdf unchanged.
assertions: A new PDF file was created in ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/
The output filename contains a version number (v1, v2, etc.)
The output filename contains the company name or job title
The original resume.pdf was not modified
The chat response includes a STRENGTHS section with at least 2 bullets
The chat response includes a GAPS section with at least 2 bullets
The chat response includes a SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS section with at least 2 bullets
The chat response does NOT contain a Key Changes section
The resume content was NOT dumped into the chat response
No skills or experiences were fabricated that aren't in the original resume

evals.prompt:
hey can you tailor my resume for this ML role? my resume is the docx file.

ML Engineer — Series B AI Startup (San Francisco)

What you'll do:
- Design and deploy production ML pipelines for our core product
- Fine-tune and evaluate LLMs for domain-specific tasks
- Build and maintain RAG systems for knowledge retrieval
- Collaborate with product and engineering to ship ML features fast

Requirements:
- 2+ years building production ML pipelines
- Experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow
- Familiarity with LLMs and prompt engineering
- Strong Python skills
- Experience deploying models to production (Docker, K8s preferred)
- BS/MS in CS or related field

Nice to have:
- Experience with RAG systems
- Contributions to open source
- Startup experience
evals.expected_output: A new versioned DOCX file saved to ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/. Chat output with STRENGTHS, GAPS, SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS only. Jordan Rivera's ML background should align well with this role.
assertions: A new DOCX file was created in ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/
The output filename contains a version number
The original DOCX was not modified
The chat response includes a STRENGTHS section
The chat response includes a GAPS section
The chat response includes a SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS section
The chat response does NOT contain a Key Changes section
Strengths reference specific ML skills from both the resume and job description
Gaps identify specific job requirements not covered in the resume
The resume content was NOT dumped into the chat response

evals.prompt:
Tailor my LaTeX resume (resume.tex) for this role:

Software Engineer II at Microsoft — Cloud + AI Platform

Responsibilities:
- Design and implement scalable cloud services on Azure
- Build microservices that handle millions of requests per day
- Write clean, tested, production-quality code in C# or Java
- Participate in on-call rotations and incident response
- Collaborate across teams to define technical roadmaps

Required qualifications:
- 3+ years professional software development experience
- Strong proficiency in C#, .NET, or Java
- Experience with cloud services (Azure preferred, AWS/GCP acceptable)
- Understanding of distributed systems and microservices architecture
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills
- BS in Computer Science or equivalent

Preferred qualifications:
- Experience with Kubernetes or container orchestration
- Familiarity with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd)
- Experience with observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog)
evals.expected_output: A new versioned .tex file saved to ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/. The .tex file should be valid LaTeX. Chat output with STRENGTHS, GAPS, SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS. Maya Chen's full-stack background partially matches — gaps likely around C#/.NET/Azure.
assertions: A new .tex file was created in ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/
The output .tex file is valid LaTeX (contains \begin{document} and \end{document})
The original resume.tex was not modified
The chat response includes STRENGTHS, GAPS, and SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS sections
The chat response does NOT contain a Key Changes section
The analysis mentions C# or .NET or Azure as a gap
The tailored resume preserves LaTeX document structure
No fabricated experiences or skills were added

evals.prompt: tailor my resume for this job. resume is attached.
evals.expected_output: The skill should ask for the missing job description or job link before proceeding, since only a resume was provided without a target job.
assertions: The agent asks for the missing job description or job URL
The agent does NOT proceed to tailor without a job target
No output file is created prematurely

evals.prompt:
I found this amazing Data Scientist role at Netflix. Here's the description:

Data Scientist — Content Analytics at Netflix

The Role:
- Build statistical models to optimize content acquisition and production decisions
- Design and analyze A/B tests for product features
- Partner with content strategy teams to deliver data-driven insights

Requirements:
- MS/PhD in Statistics, CS, Economics, or related quantitative field
- 3+ years experience in data science or applied statistics
- Expert in Python and R for statistical analysis
- Experience with causal inference and experimental design
- Strong SQL skills
- Experience with big data tools (Spark, Hive)
- Excellent communication skills for non-technical stakeholders

Can you help me tailor my application?
evals.expected_output: The skill should ask for the missing resume file before proceeding.
assertions: The agent asks for the missing resume file
The agent does NOT proceed to tailor without a resume
No output file is created prematurely

evals.prompt:
Tailor my resume for this:

Backend Developer at a startup. Python, APIs, databases. 2+ years.

My resume is resume.pdf.
evals.expected_output: The skill should still work with this sparse job description. Should produce a tailored resume and analysis without refusing or asking for more details.
assertions: A new PDF file was created in ~/Desktop/tailored_resumes/
The agent did NOT refuse or ask for a more detailed job description
The chat response includes STRENGTHS, GAPS, and SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS sections
The chat response does NOT contain a Key Changes section

evals.prompt:
I need a freshly tailored resume. Resume: resume.pdf. Job: Senior Data Engineer at Stripe.

Requirements:
- 5+ years of experience in data engineering
- Strong proficiency in Python and SQL
- Experience with ETL pipelines, Spark, cloud data warehouses
- BS/MS in CS

Note: I already have a v1 of this tailored resume. Generate a new version.
evals.expected_output: A new versioned file (v2 or higher) should be created even though a previous version exists. The agent should not skip or refuse.
assertions: A new PDF file was created (not skipped because a previous version exists)
The output filename contains a version number higher than v1
The agent did NOT refuse or say the resume was already tailored
The chat response includes STRENGTHS, GAPS, and SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS sections
